title: Structured output from LLM + prompt tuning
steps: Step 1: Design JSON schema + Anthropic tool definition (presentation_metadata tool with phase, messageType, suggestions, slides, qualityScore). Define 5 SlideData types: TitleSlide, ContentSlide, CodeSlide, MetricsSlide, ClosingSlide — each with slideId. Add all types to src/shared/types.ts.
Step 2 [PARALLEL A]: Model upgrade — change model to claude-sonnet-4-6, bump max_tokens to 4096, add tools array + tool_choice to API call in src/server/main.ts L285-290.
Step 3 [PARALLEL A]: DB migration + schema — add nullable metadata jsonb column to messages table in schema.ts L23-38. Update storage class in main.ts L126-233.

Step 4 [PARALLEL A]: System prompt rewrite — rewrite SYSTEM_PROMPT in main.ts L19-120. Collaborative tone, 3-4 exchanges per phase, tool-use instructions, 5 slide layout docs (~2600 tokens, cacheable), slideId stability instructions, phase pacing guidance.
Step 5: Storage layer — update addMessageToConversation (L138) to accept/save metadata. Update getConversation mapping (L180) to return metadata. Null metadata for user messages.

Step 6: Dual-mode streaming — rework stream handler L285-320. Keep text events streaming to client. Add inputJson event accumulation. On end: parse metadata, save text+metadata, send metadata SSE event then done event. Fallback for non-tool-use responses.
Step 7: Conversation history optimization — update sanitizeMessages L240-250 to strip metadata before sending to Anthropic. Only include latest slides as context note, not historical copies.
Step 8 [PARALLEL B]: SSE parser — update streamMsg() in requests.ts L96-112 to handle type:metadata events. Add onMetadata callback. Wire in App.tsx createMessage.
Step 9 [PARALLEL B]: Error handling — try/catch on JSON.parse for tool_use. Check stop_reason for max_tokens truncation warning. Backward compat for null metadata conversations.
acceptance_criteria: Sonnet 4.6 responds with streamed text AND structured metadata via tool_use
Text still streams word-by-word to client (no UX regression)
Metadata (phase, messageType, suggestions, slides, qualityScore) stored in DB jsonb column
Frontend receives metadata after stream completes via onMetadata callback
Fallback: non-tool-use responses save with null metadata (today's behavior)
Backward compat: old conversations with null metadata still load and render
Prompt feels collaborative — fewer repetitive questions, punchier 3-4 exchange phases
Suggestions array has 2-3 options per response
Slide data uses typed JSON schemas with slideId for stability tracking
Conversation history doesn't bloat with duplicate slide decks
max_tokens truncation detected and warned to client

risks.detail: stream.on('text') is blind to tool_use blocks. Must listen for both text and inputJson events.
risks.mitigation: Dual-mode: text events stream, inputJson accumulates silently, parse on end.

risks.detail: JSON + text can push toward 4096 in structure/refine with full decks.
risks.mitigation: Monitor stop_reason. Instruct model to keep slides tight. Warn client on truncation.

risks.area: Slide content drift on full-deck regeneration
risks.detail: Model may subtly rephrase unchanged slides when regenerating full deck.
risks.mitigation: System prompt stability instruction. Only latest slides in context. Client-side diff by slideId.

risks.detail: ~200-400 tokens for tool def, but cacheable with prompt caching.

context.model_upgrade: Haiku 4.5 -> Sonnet 4.6 for reliable tool use. ~$51/mo at 50 users.
context.slide_format: Model outputs typed JSON (not HTML). Frontend renders JSON to HTML via CSS design system.
context.user_feedback: Feels like interrogation, wants suggestion buttons, markdown, punchier flow.
context.key_decision: Dual-mode streaming: text streams live, tool_use metadata arrives at end.
